title: CLAUDE.md — monecascale
text: monecascale sibling package moneca hosts scalable clustering backends mobility network analysis. extends moneca beyond classification-scale matrices (dozens hundred categories) toward settings person × employer mobility (10^6 × 10^5). Every exported entry point returns moneca-class S3 object, moneca’s analysis plotting stack consumes output unchanged.

title: Auto Level Selection with auto_segment_levels()
text: auto_segment_levels() closes recurring loose end hierarchical segmentation: backend returns full nested hierarchy, downstream analysis typically needs single level. Picking level hand brittle, scale, difficult justify reproducible pipeline. function implements two principled, purely post-hoc criteria picking preferred level fitted hierarchy. composes directly two scalable backends package: moneca_sbm() (direction D2) - hierarchical DC-SBM square mobility. moneca_bipartite() (direction D1) - hierarchical DC-LBM rectangular mobility, picked independently row column sides. Plain moneca::moneca_fast() output yet supported rejected informative error; hook deferred monecascale 0.3.1. Two entry points exist. function takes fitted object returns pick plus per-level diagnostics. segment.levels = "auto" argument moneca_sbm() moneca_bipartite() wrapper sugar: fit full hierarchy, pick level, trim returned object level one call.

title: Auto Level Selection with auto_segment_levels()
text: MDL elbow. criterion walks per-level Minimum Description Length (MDL) series (coarse fine) picks point maximum perpendicular distance chord connecting two endpoints. Kneedle heuristic (Satopaa et al. 2011) default. alternative mdl_elbow = "max_second_diff" selects discrete second-difference peak, sensitive short hierarchies noisier long ones. moneca_sbm() MDL series -ICL greed::DcSbm; moneca_bipartite() per-side series -joint_icl greed::DcLbm, sliced side hand. cases lower better, Kneedle detects knee decreasing curve. MI plateau. criterion computes level--level mutual information (m_l, m_{l+1}) adjacent hierarchy levels directly segment.list, independently backend diagnostics. (m_l, m_{l+1}) measures much structure level l+1 shares coarser level l: high refinement consistent coarser level drops refinement begins reshuffle memberships. plateau first level whose gain I_l - I_{l-1} falls plateau_tol * max() - resolution refinement adds little therefore heuristically redundant. plateau detected finest level returned. two criteria use different inputs (MDL series vs. membership vectors), can often disagree. disagreement diagnostic, illustrated section 6.

title: Auto Level Selection with auto_segment_levels()
text: moneca_sbm() moneca_bipartite() accept segment.levels = "auto". wrapper fits full hierarchy, delegates auto_segment_levels(), trims returned object picked level. picker result attached callers can still inspect happened. trimmed object regular moneca-class object: moneca::segment.*() moneca::plot_moneca_*() APIs run unchanged. auto_method = "mi_plateau" accepted symmetrically dispatches MI criterion inside wrapper.
code: fit_auto <- monecascale::moneca_sbm(   mob,   backend = "greed",   seed = 2026,   segment.levels = "auto",   auto_method = "mdl" )  # The full-hierarchy fit would have returned length(segment.list) levels; # after trimming we see only those up to the pick. length(fit_auto$segment.list) #> [1] 11  # The picker result is retained for auditability. fit_auto$auto_level #> <auto_segment_levels> method=mdl backend=sbm picked level=11 of 16 #>  level n_blocks       mdl mi_to_next     score #>      2       17  793.0154         NA  793.0154 #>      3       16  826.1811         NA  826.1811 #>      4       15  856.5932         NA  856.5932 #>      5       14  892.5546         NA  892.5546 #>      6       13  933.7947         NA  933.7947 #>      7       12  974.8118         NA  974.8118 #>      8       11 1019.7153         NA 1019.7153 #>      9       10 1085.4879         NA 1085.4879 #>     10        9 1206.5826         NA 1206.5826 #>     11        8 1371.6359         NA 1371.6359 #>     12        7 1589.5938         NA 1589.5938 #>     13        6 1806.0638         NA 1806.0638 #>     14        5 2027.6486         NA 2027.6486 #>     15        4 2244.2453         NA 2244.2453 #>     16        3 2592.2005         NA 2592.2005 #>     17        2 3534.6014         NA 3534.6014  # The object is otherwise a plain moneca-class object. class(fit_auto) #> [1] "moneca"

title: Auto Level Selection with auto_segment_levels()
text: bipartite fit two sides clustered jointly exposed two one-mode projections. auto_segment_levels() recurses $rows $cols, runs criterion independently side, returns named list. two sides may land different levels. wrapper form behaves way SBM, except trimming applied per side:
code: # 1. rectangular fixture with planted row / column blocks ----- set.seed(2026) n_row <- 60 n_col <- 40 row_block <- rep(seq_len(4), length.out = n_row) col_block <- rep(seq_len(3), length.out = n_col) lambda <- outer(row_block, col_block, function(g, h) {   ifelse((g - 1) %% 3 + 1 == h, 6, 1) }) mx <- matrix(stats::rpois(n_row * n_col, lambda = lambda), n_row, n_col) rownames(mx) <- paste0("R", seq_len(n_row)) colnames(mx) <- paste0("C", seq_len(n_col))  # 2. full bipartite fit ----- bip_fit <- monecascale::moneca_bipartite(mx, seed = 2026, verbose = FALSE) bip_res <- monecascale::auto_segment_levels(bip_fit, method = "mdl")  # Two independent picks, one per side. names(bip_res) #> [1] "rows" "cols" bip_res$rows #> <auto_segment_levels> method=mdl backend=bipartite_rows picked level=3 of 4 #>  level n_blocks      mdl mi_to_next    score #>      2        3 62529.90         NA 62529.90 #>      3        2 63607.83         NA 63607.83 #>      4        2 63588.51         NA 63588.51 #>      5        1 64976.75         NA 64976.75 bip_res$cols #> <auto_segment_levels> method=mdl backend=bipartite_cols picked level=5 of 4 #>  level n_blocks      mdl mi_to_next    score #>      2        3 62529.90         NA 62529.90 #>      3        3 63607.83         NA 63607.83 #>      4        2 63588.51         NA 63588.51 #>      5        2 64976.75         NA 64976.75 bip_auto <- monecascale::moneca_bipartite(   mx,   seed = 2026,   verbose = FALSE,   segment.levels = "auto",   auto_method = "mdl" )  # Per-side picks; the two sides may disagree. bip_auto$rows$auto_level$level #> [1] 3 bip_auto$cols$auto_level$level #> [1] 5 length(bip_auto$rows$segment.list) #> [1] 3 length(bip_auto$cols$segment.list) #> [1] 5

1. prepare panels ----- mdl_df <- res_mdl$diagnostics mdl_df$criterion <- "MDL (lower is better)" mdl_df$value <- mdl_df$mdl  mi_df <- res_mi$diagnostics mi_df$criterion <- "MI to next level" mi_df$value <- mi_df$mi_to_next  plot_df <- rbind(   mdl_df[, c("level", "n_blocks", "value", "criterion")],   mi_df[, c("level", "n_blocks", "value", "criterion")] ) plot_df <- plot_df[is.finite(plot_df$value), ]  # 2. pick annotations ----- picks <- data.frame(   criterion = c("MDL (lower is better)", "MI to next level"),   level = c(res_mdl$level, res_mi$level) ) picks$value <- mapply(   function(cr, lvl) {     sub <- plot_df[plot_df$criterion == cr & plot_df$level == lvl, "value"]     if (length(sub) == 0) NA_real_ else sub[1]   },   picks$criterion,   picks$level )  # 3. stacked panel ----- ggplot2::ggplot(   plot_df,   ggplot2::aes(x = level, y = value) ) +   ggplot2::geom_line() +   ggplot2::geom_point() +   ggplot2::geom_vline(     data = picks,     mapping = ggplot2::aes(xintercept = level),     linetype = 2   ) +   ggplot2::geom_point(     data = picks[is.finite(picks$value), ],     mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = level, y = value),     colour = "#0054AD",     size = 3   ) +   ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ criterion, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "segment.list level",     y = NULL,     title = "MDL elbow vs. MI plateau on the same SBM fit"   ) +   ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

title: Bipartite Segmentation with moneca_bipartite()
text: moneca_bipartite() extends MONECA analysis stack rectangular (two-mode) mobility data, rows columns distinct entity types. Typical use cases person x employer, worker x occupation, student x school tables. square mobility matrix moneca::moneca_fast() monecascale::moneca_sbm() operate already one-mode aggregation; bipartite input preserves individual row column identity lets block model infer groups sides jointly. vignette rectangular counterpart vignette("monecascale-sbm", package = "monecascale"): square case motivates relative risk DC-SBM residual, one motivates argument carries Latent Block Model (LBM) joint row / column clustering exposed moneca’s downstream segment.*() plot_moneca_*() APIs.

what: 2. Theoretical bridge: rectangular RR as DcLbm residual
title: Bipartite Segmentation with moneca_bipartite()
text: MONECA’s weight relative-risk deviation observed mobility independence null. rectangular core O row margins r, column margins c, grand total N: RRij=OijEij,Eij=ri⋅cjN. RR_{ij} = \frac{O_{ij}}{E_{ij}}, \qquad E_{ij} = \frac{r_i \cdot c_j}{N}. degree-corrected Latent Block Model (DcLbm, Come et al. 2021) models expected edge count row row-block g column j column-block h theta_i^row * theta_j^col * omega_{g,h}, theta degree parameters omega row-block column-block interaction matrix. Setting omega -ones matrix recovers exactly (r_i c_j) / N, .e. MONECA’s independence null. DcLbm inference rectangular mobility therefore principled scalable drop-clique enumeration bipartite case, way DcSbm unipartite case covered monecascale-sbm. current backend greed::DcLbm, R-native hierarchical implementation built Integrated Classification Likelihood merge-split local search. "graphtool" backend via graph_tool.inference.minimize_blockmodel_dl(pclabel = ...) reserved scale beyond 10^5 per side.

title: Bipartite Segmentation with moneca_bipartite()
text: print.moneca_bipartite() method shows per-level table row block count, column block count, joint ICL. summary() adds backend metadata block (n_init, rr_scale, margin status).
code: library(monecascale)  # 1. synthetic rectangular fixture ----- set.seed(2026) n_row <- 60 n_col <- 40 row_block <- rep(1:4, length.out = n_row) col_block <- rep(1:3, length.out = n_col) # block-constant Poisson intensities: diagonal-ish structure lambda <- outer(row_block, col_block, function(g, h) {   ifelse((g - 1) %% 3 + 1 == h, 6, 1) }) mx <- matrix(stats::rpois(n_row * n_col, lambda = lambda), n_row, n_col) rownames(mx) <- paste0("R", seq_len(n_row)) colnames(mx) <- paste0("C", seq_len(n_col))  # 2. fit ----- out <- monecascale::moneca_bipartite(mx, seed = 2026, verbose = FALSE)  # 3. inspect ----- out #> <moneca_bipartite> rows: 60 x cols: 40, backend: greed/DcLbm, levels: 4 #>  level Krow Kcol joint_ICL #>      1    3    3 -62529.90 #>      2    2    3 -63607.83 #>      3    2    2 -63588.51 #>      4    1    2 -64976.75 summary(out) #> <moneca_bipartite> rows: 60 x cols: 40, backend: greed/DcLbm, levels: 4 #>   backend: greed   model: DcLbm   n_init: 1   rr_scale: 3 #> Hierarchy levels: #>  level Krow Kcol joint_ICL joint_MDL #>      1    3    3 -62529.90  62529.90 #>      2    2    3 -63607.83  63607.83 #>      3    2    2 -63588.51  63588.51 #>      4    1    2 -64976.75  64976.75 out$bipartite_diagnostics$n_blocks_row_per_level #> [1] 3 2 2 1 out$bipartite_diagnostics$n_blocks_col_per_level #> [1] 3 3 2 2

title: Bipartite Segmentation with moneca_bipartite()
text: moneca_bipartite() returns object class "moneca_bipartite" (disjoint "moneca") three slots: $rows, $cols, $bipartite_diagnostics. first two full moneca-class objects built one-mode projections rectangular RR. third retains joint bipartite information single square object represent. row view square, symmetric, zero-diagonal matrix n_row entities; column view square n_col. Margins appended last row column, matching moneca’s convention. diagnostics slot exposes joint structure: block_interaction_matrix_per_level list Krow x Kcol matrices, one per hierarchy level, cross-tabulating rectangular core row column memberships. Level 1 finest.

code: # 1. planted design ----- set.seed(42) n_row <- 80 n_col <- 60 K_row_true <- 4 K_col_true <- 3 row_block_true <- rep(seq_len(K_row_true), length.out = n_row) col_block_true <- rep(seq_len(K_col_true), length.out = n_col)  # 2. block-constant Poisson with strong diagonal signal ----- omega <- matrix(0.8, K_row_true, K_col_true) for (g in seq_len(K_row_true)) {   h <- ((g - 1) %% K_col_true) + 1L   omega[g, h] <- 8 } lambda <- omega[row_block_true, col_block_true] mx_parity <- matrix(   stats::rpois(n_row * n_col, lambda = lambda),   n_row,   n_col ) rownames(mx_parity) <- paste0("R", seq_len(n_row)) colnames(mx_parity) <- paste0("C", seq_len(n_col))  # 3. fit ----- fit_parity <- monecascale::moneca_bipartite(   mx_parity,   seed = 42,   verbose = FALSE ) # Minimal ARI. Returns 1 when labelings agree up to permutation. ari <- function(a, b) {   tab <- table(a, b)   n <- sum(tab)   sc <- function(x) sum(choose(x, 2))   a_sum <- sc(rowSums(tab))   b_sum <- sc(colSums(tab))   tab_sum <- sc(as.vector(tab))   expected <- a_sum * b_sum / choose(n, 2)   max_idx <- (a_sum + b_sum) / 2   if (max_idx == expected) return(1)   (tab_sum - expected) / (max_idx - expected) }  level_labels <- function(mon, level) {   n_core <- nrow(mon$mat.list[[1]]) - 1L   labels <- rep(NA_integer_, n_core)   cliques <- mon$segment.list[[level]]   for (b in seq_along(cliques)) labels[cliques[[b]]] <- b   na_idx <- is.na(labels)   if (any(na_idx)) {     labels[na_idx] <- max(0L, labels, na.rm = TRUE) + seq_len(sum(na_idx))   }   as.integer(labels) } # 1. pick the level whose row-block count matches the planted K_row ----- krow_per_level <- fit_parity$bipartite_diagnostics$n_blocks_row_per_level kcol_per_level <- fit_parity$bipartite_diagnostics$n_blocks_col_per_level row_level <- which.min(abs(krow_per_level - K_row_true)) + 1L col_level <- which.min(abs(kcol_per_level - K_col_true)) + 1L  # 2. extract moneca-style node labels at those levels ----- row_hat <- level_labels(fit_parity$rows, row_level) col_hat <- level_labels(fit_parity$cols, col_level)  # 3. score against planted memberships ----- ari_row <- ari(row_block_true, row_hat) ari_col <- ari(col_block_true, col_hat) data.frame(   side = c("rows", "cols"),   K_true = c(K_row_true, K_col_true),   K_hat = c(krow_per_level[row_level - 1L], kcol_per_level[col_level - 1L]),   ARI = round(c(ari_row, ari_col), 3) ) #>   side K_true K_hat   ARI #> 1 rows      4     3 0.706 #> 2 cols      3     3 1.000

title: Out-of-core RR computation with DuckDB
text: RR computation three-CTE aggregation edge list. One CTE sums counts per row; one sums per column; one takes grand total. final join multiplies observed count total / (row_sum * col_sum) yield rr. ordering required, dense intermediate constructed. DuckDB streams aggregations edge list, streams join result back R long form. R side receives (row_id, col_id, count, rr) table whose length equals number non-zero cells O, feeds directly Matrix::sparseMatrix(). $margins$total reflects full input total rather post-filter total, RR denominator uses.
code: WITH row_sums AS (   SELECT row_id, SUM(w) AS s FROM edges GROUP BY row_id ), col_sums AS (   SELECT col_id, SUM(w) AS s FROM edges GROUP BY col_id ), totals AS (   SELECT SUM(w) AS total FROM edges ) SELECT   e.row_id,   e.col_id,   e.w AS count,   e.w * t.total / (r.s * c.s) AS rr FROM edges e JOIN row_sums r ON r.row_id = e.row_id JOIN col_sums c ON c.col_id = e.col_id CROSS JOIN totals t;

RR_{ij} = \frac{O_{ij}}{\bigl(r_i \cdot c_j\bigr) / T}. degree-corrected stochastic block model (Karrer & Newman, 2011) models expected number edges nodes j \theta_i \theta_j \omega_{g_i, g_j}, \theta degree parameters \omega block-interaction matrix. Setting \omega identity yields exactly (r_i c_j) / T expected count — .e., MONECA’s independence null. sense MONECA’s RR DC-SBM residual, clique rule thresholded RR hand-rolled approximation block inference. modern DC-SBM inference routine therefore principled scalable drop-. Two backends wired behind moneca_sbm(): "greed" (default, R-native via greed::DcSbm): hierarchical dendrogram, Poisson likelihood counts, sparse dgCMatrix input end--end, portable parallel via future framework. code: ari <- function(a, b) {   tab <- table(a, b); n <- sum(tab)   sc <- function(x) sum(choose(x, 2))   a_sum <- sc(rowSums(tab)); b_sum <- sc(colSums(tab))   tab_sum <- sc(as.vector(tab))   expected <- a_sum * b_sum / choose(n, 2)   max_idx <- (a_sum + b_sum) / 2   if (max_idx == expected) return(1)   (tab_sum - expected) / (max_idx - expected) }  nmi <- function(a, b) {   tab <- table(a, b); n <- sum(tab); p <- tab / n   pa <- rowSums(p); pb <- colSums(p); nz <- p > 0   mi <- sum(p[nz] * log(p[nz] / outer(pa, pb)[nz]))   ha <- -sum(pa[pa > 0] * log(pa[pa > 0]))   hb <- -sum(pb[pb > 0] * log(pb[pb > 0]))   if (ha == 0 || hb == 0) return(0)   mi / sqrt(ha * hb) }  level_labels <- function(mon, level) {   n_core <- nrow(mon$mat.list[[1]]) - 1L   labels <- rep(NA_integer_, n_core)   cliques <- mon$segment.list[[level]]   for (b in seq_along(cliques)) labels[cliques[[b]]] <- b   na_idx <- is.na(labels)   if (any(na_idx)) {     labels[na_idx] <- max(0L, labels, na.rm = TRUE) + seq_len(sum(na_idx))   }   as.integer(labels) } fast <- moneca::moneca_fast(mob, segment.levels = 3, progress = FALSE) sbm  <- moneca_sbm(mob, backend = "greed", seed = 2026)  fast_levels <- seq.int(2L, length(fast$segment.list)) sbm_levels  <- seq.int(2L, length(sbm$segment.list))  grid <- expand.grid(fast_lvl = fast_levels, sbm_lvl = sbm_levels) grid$ari <- mapply(function(lf, ls) ari(level_labels(fast, lf),                                         level_labels(sbm, ls)),                    grid$fast_lvl, grid$sbm_lvl) grid$nmi <- mapply(function(lf, ls) nmi(level_labels(fast, lf),                                         level_labels(sbm, ls)),                    grid$fast_lvl, grid$sbm_lvl)  best <- grid[which.max(grid$ari), , drop = FALSE] best #>    fast_lvl sbm_lvl       ari      nmi #> 19        2       8 0.6709957 0.905422

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: moneca remains home reference algorithm (moneca(), moneca_fast()), weight-matrix construction, segment metadata, full visualization stack. monecascale adds alternative clustering backends whose output consumed unchanged moneca’s analysis plotting functions. Every monecascale entry point returns moneca-class S3 object extra diagnostics slot.

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: Hierarchical degree-corrected stochastic block model (DC-SBM) clustering. Two dispatchable backends: "greed" (default, R-native via greed::DcSbm) — sparse dgCMatrix end--end, hierarchical dendrogram via cut(sol, k), portable parallel via future. Scales ~104–105 nodes. "graphtool" (opt-, Python via reticulate) — Peixoto’s minimize_nested_blockmodel_dl(), MDL-based level selection. Scales 10^6+ nodes. Theoretical rationale. moneca’s RR = O / E DC-SBM residual identity block interaction; DC-SBM inference therefore principled scalable drop-moneca’s clique step. Empirical parity 20-class synthetic fixture: ARI = 0.671, NMI = 0.905 vs moneca::moneca_fast().

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: Degree-corrected Latent Block Model (DcLbm) rectangular person × employer (worker × occupation) mobility matrices. Output comprises two linked moneca-class objects ($rows, $cols) consumable moneca’s full analysis plotting stack. Scales ~104–105 rows/columns per side.

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: Flow-based hierarchical clustering via Infomap Map Equation. Backend via igraph::cluster_infomap() (R-native, scales ~10^5 nodes). recursive-flat wrapper synthesises 2–3 level hierarchy. Returns moneca-class object $flow_diagnostics (codelength per level, node assignments).

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: Post-hoc hierarchy-level auto-selection full hierarchical fit. Two criteria: method = "mdl" — MDL elbow detection via kneedle algorithm (Satopää et al. 2011), max_second_diff fallback. method = "mi_plateau" — mutual-information plateau: picks level marginal MI gain drops threshold maximum MI. Available segment.levels = "auto" moneca_sbm(), moneca_bipartite(), moneca_flow() one-shot hierarchy fitting trimming. Recognises backend-specific diagnostics (MDL SBM flow; mutual information bipartite).

title: Scalable Backends for MONECA Mobility Clustering
text: --core sparse relative-risk matrix construction via DuckDB SQL. Accepts edge-list input data.frame, file path (.csv / .parquet), table name pre-connected DuckDB instance. Returns monecascale_rr list sparse $rr, sparse $counts, margin metadata. Composes seamlessly moneca_bipartite() (via $rr) moneca_sbm() / moneca_flow() (via $counts).

title: Auto-select a Hierarchy Level for a `moneca` Fit — auto_segment_levels
text: `"mdl"` criterion fetches per-level MDL series `obj$sbm_diagnostics$mdl_per_level` (SBM backends) slim per-side slice `obj$bipartite_diagnostics_side$joint_mdl_per_level` (`$rows` `$cols` child `moneca_bipartite` object). series reordered coarse--fine (ascending block count) elbow selected either Kneedle heuristic discrete second-difference peak. `"mi_plateau"` criterion computes level--level sample mutual information `obj$segment.list` directly (reliance backend diagnostics). picks smallest level gain \(I_l - I_{l-1}\) falls `plateau_tol * max()`; plateau detected, returns finest level. `moneca::moneca_fast()` output function errors : neither MDL series principled MI plateau available without per-iteration trace, upstream `moneca_fast()` emit. Support planned monecascale 0.3.1.

title: Flow-based (Infomap / Map Equation) Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_flow
text: `igraph::cluster_infomap()` returns flat partition. expose hierarchy compatible moneca's multi-level segment contract, recursive wrapper re-runs flat algorithm module's induced subgraph level 2, optionally level 3 `depth = 3L`. *approximate* hierarchy: jointly optimise single hierarchical map equation. joint-MDL optimisation use standalone C++ Infomap binary (planned optional backend future release). Input raw mobility counts. relative-risk transformation applied; users want RR-weighted flow clustering preprocess matrix , use [moneca_sbm()] consumes RR natively via DC-SBM bridge. codelength per level computed directly map equation full graph (PageRank-based stationary distribution plus module exit probabilities), inferred sub-module Infomap runs. ensures `codelength_per_level` comparable across levels recursive hierarchy.

title: Flow-based (Infomap / Map Equation) Backend for MONECA Segmentation — moneca_flow
code: if (requireNamespace("igraph", quietly = TRUE)) {   # Small planted-community fixture   set.seed(42)   n <- 20   block <- rep(1:4, each = 5)   mx <- matrix(rpois(n * n, 0.2), n, n)   for (k in 1:4) {     idx <- which(block == k)     mx[idx, idx] <- mx[idx, idx] + rpois(length(idx)^2, 3)   }   diag(mx) <- 0   fit <- moneca_flow(mx, depth = 2L, seed = 1L)   fit$flow_diagnostics$codelength_per_level } #> [1] 3.766350 3.308539

"auto" prefers graphtool available matrix exceeds 5000 nodes; otherwise uses greed. deg_corr Logical. TRUE (default), fits degree-corrected SBM. Set FALSE plain SBM. edge_model One "poisson" (default, appropriate count-valued mobility) "bernoulli" (binary edges). small.cell.reduction Numeric. Cells core matrix value zeroed SBM inference. Mirrors moneca::moneca_fast() parameter. symmetric_method One "sum" (default; mx + t(mx)), "min" (2 * pmin(mx, t(mx)), -weights one-way flows), "none" (keep directed). Applied core SBM. segment.levels Integer, NULL, string "auto". NULL (default), hierarchy levels produced backend (K_final 2) returned. integer, hierarchy truncated many approximately log-spaced levels. "auto", full hierarchy fit auto_segment_levels picks single preferred level via auto_method; object trimmed level carries picker result $auto_level. auto_method One "mdl" (default) "mi_plateau". Ignored unless segment.levels = "auto". max_K Integer NULL. Upper bound number blocks finest level (greed backend ). Defaults max(20, n_core / 5). isolates Logical. TRUE, attaches $isolates_summary slot mirroring moneca::moneca_fast(). has_margins One "auto" (default), TRUE, FALSE. Controls whether detect /append row/column margins input matrix. seed Integer seed reproducible inference. n_init Integer. Number independent backend runs; best-scoring fit retained. Defaults 1 greed (quasi-deterministic) 5 typical graphtool (MCMC). verbose Logical. TRUE, prints backend progress. ... Additional arguments forwarded backend.

what: Build a Sparse Relative-Risk Matrix Out-of-Core via DuckDB — rr_from_duckdb
title: Build a Sparse Relative-Risk Matrix Out-of-Core via DuckDB — rr_from_duckdb
text: Aggregates edge list sparse relative-risk (`RR = O / E`) matrix plus companion raw-count matrix, using DuckDB aggregation engine. R side never allocates dense `n_rows x n_cols` matrix, function scales person x employer worker x occupation mobility tables OOM `moneca::weight.matrix()`.

title: Build a Sparse Relative-Risk Matrix Out-of-Core via DuckDB — rr_from_duckdb
code: rr_from_duckdb(   edges,   row_col = "row",   col_col = "col",   weight_col = "weight",   con = NULL,   bipartite = FALSE,   min_count = 0,   small_cell_reduction = 0,   zero_diagonal = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   ... )

title: Build a Sparse Relative-Risk Matrix Out-of-Core via DuckDB — rr_from_duckdb
text: edges One : () -memory `data.frame` / `data.table` columns named `row_col`, `col_col`, `weight_col`; (b) single character file path ending `.parquet`, `.csv`, `.csv.gz`; (c) character scalar naming pre-existing DuckDB table / view supplied `con`. row_col, col_col, weight_col Column names inside source. Defaults `"row"`, `"col"`, `"weight"`. con Optional `DBIConnection` DuckDB. Required `edges` bare table name. `NULL`, transient -memory DuckDB connection opened call closed exit. bipartite Logical. `FALSE` (default), row column identifiers live namespace unioned shared factor, yielding square output identical row column names. `TRUE`, identifiers disjoint output rectangular separate row / column names. min_count Numeric. Drop edges `w < min_count` RR computation. Defaults `0` (filter). small_cell_reduction Numeric. Drop rows columns whose marginal sum threshold RR computation. Mirrors `small.cell.reduction` argument `moneca::moneca_fast()`. Defaults `0` (filter). zero_diagonal Logical. `TRUE` (default) `bipartite = FALSE`, self-loops (`row_id == col_id`) dropped RR computation. Ignored `bipartite = TRUE`. verbose Logical. `TRUE`, message number non-zero RR cells returned. ... Reserved forward compatibility.

title: Build a Sparse Relative-Risk Matrix Out-of-Core via DuckDB — rr_from_duckdb
text: list class `"monecascale_rr"` elements   * `rr` - sparse `dgCMatrix` observed / expected; one entry per     non-zero input cell.   * `counts` - sparse `dgCMatrix` raw observed counts;     sparsity pattern `rr`.   * `margins` - list numeric vectors `row` `col`, plus     scalar `total`.   * `node_names` - list character vectors `row` `col`.     Identical `bipartite = FALSE`.   * `bipartite` - logical echoing input.   * `n_edges` - integer count input rows read.   * `n_nonzero` - integer number non-zero cells `rr`.

title: Build a Sparse Relative-Risk Matrix Out-of-Core via DuckDB — rr_from_duckdb
text: RR aggregation pushed single SQL query template “`sql row_m (SELECT row_id, SUM(w) s edges GROUP row_id),      col_m (SELECT col_id, SUM(w) s edges GROUP col_id),      tot   (SELECT SUM(w) total edges) SELECT e.row_id, e.col_id, e.w count,        e.w * tot.total / (r.s * c.s) rr edges e JOIN row_m r USING (row_id) JOIN col_m c USING (col_id) CROSS JOIN tot e.w > 0; “` join--divide pattern emits rows whose edge weight non-zero, sparsity preserved end end: result set one row per non-zero input edge, R-side `Matrix::sparseMatrix()` call materialises directly `dgCMatrix`. `bipartite = FALSE`, row column IDs unioned single factor output matrices square identical dimnames. `bipartite = TRUE`, row column namespaces stay disjoint output rectangular. Preprocessing applied order: optional small-cell reduction (drops rows/cols whose marginal sum falls threshold), optional `min_count` filter (drops individual edges), optional zero-diagonal filter (active `bipartite = FALSE`). three filters applied SQL.

title: monecascale 0.2.0
text: moneca_bipartite() — D1 scaling roadmap. Accepts rectangular person × employer (worker × occupation, etc.) mobility matrices returns S3 object class "moneca_bipartite" wrapping two linked moneca-class objects ($rows, $cols) plus $bipartite_diagnostics slot carrying joint ICL/MDL trace, per-level block counts per side, rectangular RR, per-level Krow × Kcol block-interaction matrices. Single backend: greed::DcLbm (hierarchical Poisson latent block model). Scales ~104–105 rows / cols per side. Output contract: $rows $cols consumable unchanged moneca::segment.membership(), moneca::segment.quality(), moneca::plot_moneca_ggraph(), moneca::plot_moneca_hierarchical().

title: monecascale 0.2.0
text: Rectangular RR = O / ((row · col) / total) DcLbm residual identity block interaction, extending RR ≡ DC-SBM bridge D2. moneca_bipartite() fits DcLbm scaled-rounded RR projects side via one-mode RR aggregation D_other_margin^{-1} normalisation.

title: monecascale 0.1.0
text: "greed" (default, R-native via greed::DcSbm): hierarchical dendrogram, Poisson likelihood, sparse dgCMatrix end--end, portable parallel via future framework. Scales ~104–105 nodes. "graphtool" (opt-, Python via reticulate): Peixoto’s minimize_nested_blockmodel_dl() automatic MDL-based level selection. Scales 10^6+ nodes.
